title: eriogonum corymbosum var. glutinosum
intro: Eriogonum corymbosum var. glutinosum, also known as sticky buckwheat, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States. The sticky buckwheat has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, providing various health benefits such as antioxidant and antibacterial effects, which can help protect against infections and promote overall well-being. Eriogonum corymbosum var. glutinosum can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
